title: Multiple instance in  a single module instantiation
body:
---


Author Name: **Franck Jullien** (@fjullien)
Original Redmine Issue: 884 from https://www.veripool.org
Original Date: 2015-02-19
Original Assignee: Wilson Snyder (@wsnyder)

---

The verilog standard says that "One or more module instances (identical copies of a module) can be specified in a single module instantiation statement.".
So we can have for example:

```
  fifo4 #(8)
  rfifo(
	.clk   ( clk_i   ),
	.rst   ( ~rst_i  ),
	.clr   ( ~spe    ),
	.din   ( treg    ),
	.we    ( rfwe    ),
	.dout  ( rfdout  ),
	.re    ( rfre    ),
	.full  ( rffull  ),
	.empty ( rfempty )
  ),
  wfifo(
	.clk   ( clk_i   ),
	.rst   ( ~rst_i  ),
	.clr   ( ~spe    ),
	.din   ( dat_i   ),
	.we    ( wfwe    ),
	.dout  ( wfdout  ),
	.re    ( wfre    ),
	.full  ( wffull  ),
	.empty ( wfempty )
  );

```
code_blocks:
fifo4 #(8)
  rfifo(
	.clk   ( clk_i   ),
	.rst   ( ~rst_i  ),
	.clr   ( ~spe    ),
	.din   ( treg    ),
	.we    ( rfwe    ),
	.dout  ( rfdout  ),
	.re    ( rfre    ),
	.full  ( rffull  ),
	.empty ( rfempty )
  ),
  wfifo(
	.clk   ( clk_i   ),
	.rst   ( ~rst_i  ),
	.clr   ( ~spe    ),
	.din   ( dat_i   ),
	.we    ( wfwe    ),
	.dout  ( wfdout  ),
	.re    ( wfre    ),
	.full  ( wffull  ),
	.empty ( wfempty )
  );
